Monster Pilot

Dominik Graetz

University of Oregon

2026-03-10

1 Reading in data

library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.5
## ✔ forcats   1.0.0     ✔ stringr   1.5.1
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.0     ✔ tibble    3.2.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
## ✔ purrr     1.0.4     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(rio)
source("current_model.R")

files <- list.files("../data/", pattern = "txt", full.names = TRUE)

data <- lapply(files, function(x){
  import(x) %>% 
    mutate(ID = str_extract(x, "[[:digit:]]+(?=_monsters)") %>% factor(), 
           .before = Block)
}) %>%
  bind_rows()

data <- data %>%
  filter(Is_practice == 0) %>%
  filter(!is.nan(RT_task)) %>%
  filter(Trial > 1) %>%
  group_by(ID, Block) %>%
  mutate(Task_rew = Correct * A_Win,
         missed_factor = ifelse(last_trial_on_state == 1 & Detected_current_state == 0, 1, 0),
         Monster_loss =  missed_factor * B_Loss,
         current_reward = Task_rew - Monster_loss,
         current_reward = cumsum(current_reward)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(ITI = ifelse(ID == 100, 0.1, ITI),
         Placeh = ifelse(ID == 100, 0, Placeh),
         Placeh = factor(Placeh),
         ITI = factor(ITI),
         B_Loss = factor(B_Loss),
         ID = factor(ID))


agg_ID <- data %>%
  group_by(ID, Block, B_Loss, ITI, Placeh) %>%
  summarize(RT_task_checked = mean(RT_task[State_checked == 1]),
            RT_task_nc = mean(RT_task[State_checked == 0]),
            Check = mean(State_checked),
            empirical_reward = current_reward[n()]) %>%
  group_by(ID, B_Loss, ITI, Placeh) %>%
  summarize(RT_task_checked_M = mean(RT_task_checked),
            RT_task_checked_SD= sd(RT_task_checked),
            RT_task_nc_M = mean(RT_task_nc),
            RT_task_nc_SD = sd(RT_task_nc),
            Check = mean(Check),
            abs_cost = RT_task_checked_M - RT_task_nc_M,
            empirical_reward = mean(empirical_reward))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'ID', 'Block', 'B_Loss', 'ITI'. You can
## override using the `.groups` argument.
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'ID', 'B_Loss', 'ITI'. You can override
## using the `.groups` argument.

2 Optimality surfaces

library(expm)
## Loading required package: Matrix
## 
## Attaching package: 'Matrix'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:tidyr':
## 
##     expand, pack, unpack
## 
## Attaching package: 'expm'
## The following object is masked from 'package:Matrix':
## 
##     expm
max_trials <- 90/(agg_ID$RT_task_nc_M %>% min())


st <- xfun::cache_rds({
  get_states(p_A2B = 0.1, p_B2A = 0.1, cr_a = seq(0, 1, by = 0.01), cr_b = seq(0, 1, by = 0.01), N_Trials = max_trials)
}, file = "states.RDS", rerun = 0)

#get_payout(states = st, RT_c = 1.5, RT_nc = .6, ITI = 1, Win = 1, Loss = 30, TTime = 90)


agg_ID$payoff <- NA

for (i in 1:nrow(agg_ID)){
  print(i)
  agg_ID$payoff[i] = list(get_payout(states = st, 
                                     RT_c = agg_ID$RT_task_checked_M[i], 
                                     RT_nc = agg_ID$RT_task_nc_M[i], 
                                     ITI = as.numeric(as.character(agg_ID$ITI[i])), 
                                     Win = 1, 
                                     Loss = as.numeric(as.character(agg_ID$B_Loss[i])), 
                                     TTime = 90)) 
}
## [1] 1
## [1] 2
## [1] 3
## [1] 4
## [1] 5
## [1] 6

## [1] 8

## [1] 44
## [1] 45
## [1] 46
## [1] 47
## [1] 48
## [1] 49
## [1] 50
## [1] 51
agg_ID2 <- agg_ID %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(group = 1:n()) %>%
  unnest(payoff)


agg_ID2_by_state <- data %>%
  group_by(ID, Block) %>%
  mutate(check_group = cumsum(State_checked)) %>%
  group_by(ID, Block, check_group) %>%
  mutate(state_at_check = StateOn[1]) %>%
  filter(check_group != 0) %>%
  group_by(ID, Block, B_Loss, ITI, Placeh, check_group, state_at_check) %>%  
  summarize(avg_run_length = n()) %>%
  group_by(ID, Block, B_Loss, ITI, Placeh, state_at_check) %>%
  summarize(avg_run_length = mean(avg_run_length)) %>%
  group_by(ID, B_Loss, ITI, Placeh, state_at_check) %>%
  summarize(avg_run_length = mean(avg_run_length),
            cc = 1/avg_run_length) %>%
  mutate(state_at_check = factor(state_at_check)) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = state_at_check, values_from = cc, id_cols = c(ID, B_Loss, Placeh, ITI)) %>%
  rename("cr_a_empirical" = `1`, "cr_b_empirical" = `0`)
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'ID', 'Block', 'B_Loss', 'ITI', 'Placeh',
## 'check_group'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'ID', 'Block', 'B_Loss', 'ITI', 'Placeh'.
## You can override using the `.groups` argument.
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'ID', 'B_Loss', 'ITI', 'Placeh'. You can
## override using the `.groups` argument.
agg_ID2_by_state <- agg_ID2_by_state %>%
  left_join(agg_ID2) %>%
  mutate(label = paste0("ID", ID, ", Placeh", Placeh, ",\nLoss", B_Loss, ", ITI", ITI))
## Joining with `by = join_by(ID, B_Loss, Placeh, ITI)`
ggplot(agg_ID2_by_state, aes(x = cr_a, y = cr_b, fill = Rew_corrected))+
  geom_tile()+
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1)+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()+
  ggnewscale::new_scale("fill")+
  geom_point(data = agg_ID2_by_state %>% filter(is_max == 1) %>% slice(1), aes(fill = "opt"), pch = 23)+
  geom_point(data = agg_ID2_by_state %>% filter(is_max_same == 1), aes(x = cr_a_empirical, y = cr_b_empirical, fill = "actual"), pch = 23)+
  geom_point(data = agg_ID2_by_state %>% filter(is_max_same == 1), aes(fill = "opt_same_cr"), pch = 23)+
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("opt" = "red",
                                "opt_same_cr" = "orange",
                                "actual" = "blue"),
                     labels = c("opt" = "optimum",
                                "opt_same_cr" = "optimum restricted to same check rate",
                                "actual" = "actual"))+
  facet_wrap(~label) +
  theme_classic()+
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

3 How well does my model predict reward?

predicted_rewards <- agg_ID2_by_state %>%
  group_by(ID, B_Loss, Placeh, ITI) %>%
  mutate(a_dist = abs(cr_a_empirical - cr_a),
         b_dist = abs(cr_b_empirical - cr_b)) %>%
  filter(a_dist == min(a_dist) & b_dist == min(b_dist))


ggplot(predicted_rewards, aes(x = Rew_corrected, y = empirical_reward, color = ITI))+
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1)+
  geom_point()+
  labs(x = "predicted reward", 
       y = "actual reward")+
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1)+
  facet_wrap(~ID)+
  theme_classic()

# In Loss = 2, you should not check; 

# At Loss = 10
# cr_a  cr_b is_max is_max_same
# <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>       <dbl>
#   1 0.1   0.225      1           0
#   2 0.175 0.175      0           1


# At Loss = 30
# cr_a  cr_b is_max is_max_same
# <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>       <dbl>
#   1  0.25 0.575      1           0
#   2  0.45 0.45       0           1

4 All effects

agg <- agg_ID %>%
  group_by(B_Loss, Placeh, ITI) %>%
  summarize(Check = mean(Check))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'B_Loss', 'Placeh'. You can override using
## the `.groups` argument.
ggplot(agg_ID, aes(x = B_Loss, y = Check, group = interaction(ID, Placeh), color = ID, linetype = Placeh))+
  geom_line()+
  geom_line(data = agg, aes(x = B_Loss, y = Check, group = interaction(Placeh), linetype = Placeh), inherit.aes = FALSE, lwd = 1.5)+
  facet_wrap(~ITI)+
  theme_classic()

5 Loss x Placeholder

agg_ID_ph_loss <- data %>%
  group_by(ID, Placeh, B_Loss) %>%
  summarize(Check = mean(State_checked))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'ID', 'Placeh'. You can override using the
## `.groups` argument.
ggplot(agg_ID_ph_loss, aes(x = B_Loss, y = Check, group = interaction(ID, Placeh), color =ID, linetype = factor(Placeh)))+
  geom_line()+
  theme_classic()

6 ITI effect

agg_ID_iti <- data %>%
  group_by(ID, ITI) %>%
  summarize(Check = mean(State_checked))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'ID'. You can override using the `.groups`
## argument.
ggplot(agg_ID_iti, aes(x = ITI, y = Check, group = ID, color = ID))+
  geom_point()+
  geom_line()+
  theme_classic()

7 Trial sequence

Does the state probability actually converge on what the model predicts?

st_simple <- st %>%
  filter(cr_a == cr_b & cr_a == 0)

# Let's look at trial sequences

data_states_by_trial <- data %>%
  group_by(ID, Trial) %>%
  summarize(p_On = mean(StateOn),
            N = n()) %>%
  group_by(Trial) %>%
  summarize(p_On = mean(p_On),
            N = sum(N)) %>%
  mutate(p_trial_survived = N / max(N))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'ID'. You can override using the `.groups`
## argument.
ggplot(data_states_by_trial, aes(x = Trial))+
  geom_line(aes(y = p_trial_survived, color = "survival"))+
  geom_line(aes(y = p_On, color = "on"))+
  geom_line(aes(y = 1-p_On, color = "off"))+
  
  geom_line(data = st_simple, aes(x = trial, y = A_total, color = "on"), lwd = 2, alpha = 0.2)+
  geom_line(data = st_simple, aes(x = trial, y = B_total, color = "off"), lwd = 2, alpha = 0.2)+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("on" = "red",
                                "off" = "blue",
                                "survival" = "grey"),
                     labels = c("on" = "on",
                                "off" = "off",
                                "survival" = "p trials survived"),
                     name = "State")+
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1))+
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 50))+
  labs(x = "Trial",
       y = "p",
       title = "Probability of States as a function of trial number")+
  theme_classic()

8 Check Rates by information obtained during last check

check_by_state_ID <- data %>%
  group_by(ID, Block) %>%
  mutate(check_group = cumsum(State_checked)) %>%
  group_by(ID, Block, check_group) %>%
  mutate(state_at_check = StateOn[1]) %>%
  filter(check_group != 0) %>%
  group_by(ID, Block, B_Loss, check_group, state_at_check) %>%  
  summarize(avg_run_length = n()) %>%
  group_by(ID, Block, B_Loss, state_at_check) %>%
  summarize(avg_run_length = mean(avg_run_length)) %>%
  group_by(ID, B_Loss, state_at_check) %>%
  summarize(avg_run_length = mean(avg_run_length),
            cc = 1/avg_run_length) %>%
  mutate(state_at_check = factor(state_at_check))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'ID', 'Block', 'B_Loss', 'check_group'. You
## can override using the `.groups` argument.
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'ID', 'Block', 'B_Loss'. You can override
## using the `.groups` argument.
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'ID', 'B_Loss'. You can override using the
## `.groups` argument.
check_by_state_agg <- check_by_state_ID %>%
  group_by(B_Loss, state_at_check) %>%
  summarize(avg_run_length = mean(avg_run_length),
            cc = 1/avg_run_length)
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'B_Loss'. You can override using the
## `.groups` argument.
ggplot(check_by_state_ID, aes(x = state_at_check, y = cc, group = interaction(ID, B_Loss), color = B_Loss))+
  geom_line()+
  geom_line(data = check_by_state_agg, aes(group = B_Loss), lwd = 2)+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("0" = "OFF STATE",
                              "1" = "ON STATE"))+
  labs(x = "Last time I checked I saw")+
  theme_classic()

check_by_state_all_factors_ID <- data %>%
  group_by(ID, Block) %>%
  mutate(check_group = cumsum(State_checked)) %>%
  group_by(ID, Block, check_group) %>%
  mutate(state_at_check = StateOn[1]) %>%
  filter(check_group != 0) %>%
  group_by(ID, Block, B_Loss, Placeh, ITI, check_group, state_at_check) %>%  
  summarize(avg_run_length = n()) %>%
  group_by(ID, Block, B_Loss, Placeh, ITI, state_at_check) %>%
  summarize(avg_run_length = mean(avg_run_length)) %>%
  group_by(ID, B_Loss, Placeh, ITI, state_at_check) %>%
  summarize(avg_run_length = mean(avg_run_length),
            cc = 1/avg_run_length) %>%
  mutate(state_at_check = factor(state_at_check))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'ID', 'Block', 'B_Loss', 'Placeh', 'ITI',
## 'check_group'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'ID', 'Block', 'B_Loss', 'Placeh', 'ITI'.
## You can override using the `.groups` argument.
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'ID', 'B_Loss', 'Placeh', 'ITI'. You can
## override using the `.groups` argument.
check_by_state_all_factors_agg <- check_by_state_all_factors_ID %>%
  group_by(B_Loss, ITI, Placeh, state_at_check) %>%
  summarize(avg_run_length = mean(avg_run_length),
            cc = 1/avg_run_length)
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'B_Loss', 'ITI', 'Placeh'. You can override
## using the `.groups` argument.
ggplot(check_by_state_all_factors_ID, aes(x = state_at_check, y = cc, group = interaction(ID, B_Loss), color = B_Loss))+
  geom_line()+
  geom_line(data = check_by_state_all_factors_agg, aes(group = B_Loss), lwd = 2)+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("0" = "OFF STATE",
                              "1" = "ON STATE"))+
  labs(x = "Last time I checked I saw",
       title = "Expectation: Negative or flat slopes")+
  facet_grid(Placeh~ITI)+
  theme_classic()
